tier_remaining_note: Tier A remains fully cleared (0). Tier B recomputed from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model on 2026-07-18 (ROUTES_DIR fixed locally to the non-Windows path for this run only, the script file on disk still has the Windows-style path and needs a permanent fix): 483 remaining before chunk 89, minus the 50 Tier B pairs chunk 89 built, leaves 433. Not yet covered by verify_build_state.py's automatic disk reconciliation (only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are); re-run the generator manually each chunk until that gap is closed.
notes: Chunk 89 DONE (Template D, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,721 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=483 remaining before this chunk). Iceland cluster (8 routes): both directions with Hong Kong, Japan, South Africa and South Korea. Canada cluster (14 routes): both directions with Barbados, Belize, Botswana, Guyana, Namibia, Suriname and Zambia, opening Canada coverage for all seven. Turkmenistan (1 route): the missing united-states-to-turkmenistan leg, completing the pair after turkmenistan-to-united-states.md shipped in chunk 87. Albania/Algeria/Angola cluster (9 routes): both directions to Australia plus the outbound leg to the UAE for each (reverse UAE legs deliberately deferred). Australia export cluster (18 routes, Australia-to-X one direction only): Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal and Tajikistan. Genuine differentiators found: MAST's own approved-country lists (fetched direct from mast.is) place Japan in Category 1, but Hong Kong, South Africa and South Korea sit on neither Category 1 nor 2, gating those three routes behind a narrow 6-month owner-residency exception rather than a standard pathway; Japan's MAFF treats Iceland as an official 'designated region' (with Australia, NZ, Fiji, Hawaii, Guam), skipping the titre test entirely for a 180-day Iceland-resident pet. CFIA's 13 March 2025 high-risk list was checked individually per Canada-cluster country rather than assumed as a block: Barbados is not on it, Belize/Botswana/Guyana/Namibia/Suriname/Zambia all are, driving a real 7-month age floor and restricted-airport rule that Barbados pets skip entirely; CFIA's high-risk designation was confirmed to function as a commercial-import ban (dogs for resale, breeding or fostering, barred since September 2022) rather than a personal-pet barrier. Guyana's own GLDA rabies-free list does not include Canada, so a Canada-origin pet can face the same titre/quarantine review as an unlisted origin, a genuine asymmetry the page states honestly rather than assuming reciprocity. DAFF confirmed Albania, Algeria and Angola are all non-approved countries for Australian entry, each requiring a 180-day residency in a DAFF-approved staging country before RNATT and Mickleham quarantine apply; a different, source-justified staging country was picked per origin (UK for Albania, reusing its own no-EU-titre-lab gap; France for Algeria, its busiest existing air link; Portugal for Angola, TAAG's daily Luanda-Lisbon service) rather than a single reused pattern. MOCCAE's low-risk list was checked individually too: Albania is on it (no titre test), Algeria and Angola are not; Algeria's Dubai-Algiers air service agreement is unwinding and faces suspension by early 2027, flagged as a real routing risk with Qatar Airways via Doha recommended as the steadier alternative. Across the 18-country Australia-export cluster, each destination's own titre-test policy was re-verified per country rather than assumed to mirror the UK/US sibling pages: Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar and Malawi confirmed origin-blind (no titre test from anyone, Australia included); Moldova's exemption depends on Annex II Part 2 of EU Regulation 577/2013, which Australia does sit on; Montenegro applies its titre test with no origin exemption at all, including Australia, an honestly-flagged source-thin asymmetry carried over from chunk 87's note. New single-carrier or lighter routings surfaced this chunk: South African Airways' Perth-Johannesburg nonstop feeds onward to Lilongwe (Malawi) and a short Maputo hop (Mozambique); Emirates' resumed Dubai-Antananarivo service (back since 23 October 2025) gives Madagascar a two-leg path; Turkish Airlines' new Sydney/Melbourne-Istanbul services (from January 2026) open single-network routings to Kosovo, Moldova, Senegal, Tajikistan and Montenegro that didn't exist a year ago; Bahamas' BAHFSA runs a genuine two-tier system where Australia's WOAH rabies-free status skips the rabies vaccine outright, a real structural difference from the UK/US's merely rabies-controlled treatment. QA pass after the agents finished caught and fixed 14 meta descriptions over the 155-character budget, all trimmed while preserving the page-specific fact; added missing rabies_vaccination/titre_test keys to the three DAFF non-approved-country pages (Albania/Algeria/Angola-to-Australia) and a missing titre_test key to iceland-to-hong-kong.md, matching established site convention of stating 'not required'/'deferred to staging country' explicitly rather than omitting the field; zero em dashes, en dashes, HTML dash entities or curly quotes across all 50 files; zero banned vocabulary (three 'embark'/'foster' substring hits manually confirmed legitimate: embarkation/disembarkation/PetEmbark and CFIA's own commercial-dog 'fostering' definition, consistent with the same false-positive pattern logged in chunks 84 and 87); correct Marcus Webb persona and template_variant D on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser; all route_data fields populated; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk across the full 6,771-file corpus, zero broken links; zero title/description collisions against the entire corpus. Routes 6,721 -> 6,771, pages 7,527 -> 7,577. Chunk 90 (Template E, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_88: Chunk 88 DONE (Template C, Tier A + Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Closed out ALL 12 remaining Tier A pairs (Iceland x UK/US/Australia/UAE/Canada both directions, 10 routes; Macau x UAE both directions, 2 routes). Filled the remaining 38 routes with the highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs: UK/US import-side reverse legs for 16 countries (Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, both directions, 32 routes); Turkmenistan's UK leg (1 route); missing US legs for Albania, Algeria, Angola, Bahamas and Belarus (5 routes). Key finds: UK/Iceland/UAE all MAST Category 1 (no 90-day wait, mandatory 14-day quarantine still applies); US/Canada MAST Category 2; Iceland DAFF Group 2 for Australia and CFIA rabies-free for Canada; Bosnia and Herzegovina's own Annex IV list places UK in Part A, US in Part B, both titre-exempt. Routes 6,671 -> 6,721, pages 7,477 -> 7,527. Chunk 89 (Template D, Tier B) was next.
_previous_notes_chunk_87: Chunk 87 DONE (Template B, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 12 parallel research agents, one per country/cluster. 18 brand-new countries on the site (Angola, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan), each gaining both UK and US legs (36 routes); Angola additionally gained the reverse UK-to-Angola leg; Belarus gained its US leg plus the outstanding UK leg; Albania, Algeria and Bahamas each gained their US leg; Suriname gained both UAE directions; Belize and Botswana each gained their missing UAE-to-X leg; Australia-Zambia and Macau-Singapore, both directions, opened as two genuinely new pairs. Routes 6,621 -> 6,671, pages 7,427 -> 7,477. Chunk 88 (Template C, Tier A+B) was next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunks 80, 81, 82-83, 84 and 85-86 notes were dropped to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1, 3741f752, ef4d776e, ca50c52f and bd68e3c5). A rolling window of the two most recent previous-chunk notes (currently chunk 88 and chunk 87) is kept going forward.
